perturbation.original_text:
4.3 Fee Statements and Payments.
(a) Monthly and Quarterly Financial Deliverables. Exact shall on a monthly basis, not later than fifteen (15) Business Days after the end of the month, deliver to Pfizer a full suite of performance data, including Product Laboratory Services completed, average selling price per unit, Laboratory Service Revenue, rebates, net revenue and a detailed Cost of Sales schedule
perturbation.changed_text:
4.3 Fee Statements and Payments.
(a) Monthly and Quarterly Financial Deliverables. Exact shall on a monthly basis, not later than thirty (30) Business Days after the end of the month, deliver to Pfizer a full suite of performance data, including Product Laboratory Services completed, average selling price per unit, Laboratory Service Revenue, rebates, net revenue and a detailed Cost of Sales schedule
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by modifying the deadline for Exact to deliver performance data to Pfizer. Originally, Exact had to provide this data within fifteen (15) Business Days after the end of each month. The modification extends this deadline to thirty (30) Business Days. This creates an in-text contradiction with Section 4.3(b), which specifies a different deadline related to Promotion Fee payment, creating uncertainty on when is the exact deadline for Exact to delivery the data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Payment of Promotion Fee. Exact, within thirty (30) days after the end of each Calendar Quarter of the Term, shall deliver to Pfizer a consolidated report in the form of and containing the information necessary to confirm the calculation of the Promotion Fee for such Calendar Quarter, together with the underlying spreadsheets with respect to such Calendar Quarter. The Promotion Fee due with respect to such Calendar Quarter and reflected on the consolidated report shall be remitted at the time such report is made.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 4.3(a) changes the deadline for Exact to deliver performance data to Pfizer from fifteen (15) to thirty (30) Business Days. This directly conflicts with the deadline implied for providing a consolidated report for Promotion Fee calculation in Section 4.3(b), which requires delivery within thirty (30) days after the end of each calendar quarter. While both now state 30 days, the context and data requirements are different, creating ambiguity about which 30-day deadline applies to specific data deliveries and thus creates an in-text contradiction regarding when the exact deadline is for Exact to deliver data.

perturbation.original_text:
8.3 Termination for Cause. This Agreement may be terminated at any time by either Party effective:
(a) upon thirty (30) days prior written notice if the other Party fails to make the required investments pursuant to Sections 3.5 or 3.6, as applicable, or pay any amount properly due under this Agreement; provided that neither Party may terminate if the failure of the other Party to meet the investment requirements under Sections 3.5 or 3.6, as applicable, is de minimis or not material; provided, further, that any such termination shall only become effective if the allegedly breaching Party fails to remedy or cure such breach or default prior to the end of such thirty (30) day period. If, prior to the end of such thirty (30) day period, the allegedly breaching Party remedies or cures such breach or default to the reasonable satisfaction of the non-breaching Party, this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect;
perturbation.changed_text:
8.3 Termination for Cause. This Agreement may be terminated at any time by either Party effective:
(a) upon sixty (60) days prior written notice if the other Party fails to make the required investments pursuant to Sections 3.5 or 3.6, as applicable, or pay any amount properly due under this Agreement; provided that neither Party may terminate if the failure of the other Party to meet the investment requirements under Sections 3.5 or 3.6, as applicable, is de minimis or not material; provided, further, that any such termination shall only become effective if the allegedly breaching Party fails to remedy or cure such breach or default prior to the end of such thirty (30) day period. If, prior to the end of such thirty (30) day period, the allegedly breaching Party remedies or cures such breach or default to the reasonable satisfaction of the non-breaching Party, this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect;
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces a contradiction by changing the notice period required for terminating the agreement due to failure to make required investments. The original text states 'thirty (30) days' notice,' while the modified text states 'sixty (60) days prior written notice'. However, the cure period remains thirty (30) days, creating an in-text contradiction. The breaching party now has to remedy the breach before the end of the original thirty (30) day period, which is inconsistent with the sixty (60) day notice period introduced, creating ambiguity and uncertainty regarding termination proceedings. Also, what means the non-breaching party to remedy the breach, if there is only 30 days window to cure it, even if they already gave 60 days in advance? This in-text contradiction can lead to disputes about the validity and timing of any termination notice.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
8.3 Termination for Cause. This Agreement may be terminated at any time by either Party effective:
(a) upon sixty (60) days prior written notice if the other Party fails to make the required investments pursuant to Sections 3.5 or 3.6, as applicable, or pay any amount properly due under this Agreement; provided that neither Party may terminate if the failure of the other Party to meet the investment requirements under Sections 3.5 or 3.6, as applicable, is de minimis or not material; provided, further, that any such termination shall only become effective if the allegedly breaching Party fails to remedy or cure such breach or default prior to the end of such thirty (30) day period. If, prior to the end of such thirty (30) day period, the allegedly breaching Party remedies or cures such breach or default to the reasonable satisfaction of the non-breaching Party, this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect;
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the notice period for termination from thirty (30) days to sixty (60) days, while retaining the original thirty (30) day cure period. This creates a direct conflict, as the breaching party now has a sixty (60) day notice period but only thirty (30) days to cure the breach, making the cure period inconsistent with the notice period. The original text allows termination after 30 days notice, whereas the change text allows 60 days notice, yet the cure period remains at 30 days. This makes the two clauses incompatible.

perturbation.original_text: 9.5 Notices. All notices and other communications required or permitted hereunder (including any notice of force majeure, breach, termination, change of address, etc.) shall be in writing and will be deemed given (a) upon receipt if delivered personally or by facsimile transmission (receipt verified), (b) five (5) days after being deposited in the mail if mailed by registered or certified mail (return receipt requested) postage prepaid or (c) on the next Business Day if sent by overnight delivery using a nationally recognized express courier service and specifying next Business Day delivery (receipt verified), and will be sent to the Parties at the following addresses or facsimile numbers, as applicable, (or at such other address or facsimile number for a Party as will be specified by like notice; provided, however, that notices of a change of address will be effective only upon receipt thereof):
perturbation.changed_text: 9.5 Notices. All notices and other communications required or permitted hereunder (including any notice of force majeure, breach, termination, change of address, etc.) shall be in writing and will be deemed given (a) upon delivery if delivered personally or by facsimile transmission (receipt verified), (b) ten (10) days after being deposited in the mail if mailed by registered or certified mail (return receipt requested) postage prepaid or (c) two (2) Business Day if sent by overnight delivery using a nationally recognized express courier service and specifying next Business Day delivery (receipt verified), and will be sent to the Parties at the following addresses or facsimile numbers, as applicable, (or at such other address or facsimile number for a Party as will be specified by like notice; provided, however, that notices of a change of address will be effective only upon receipt thereof):
perturbation.explanation: This change modifies the timeframes for deemed delivery of notices. For mail delivery, the timeframe is extended from five (5) to ten (10) days, and for overnight delivery, it's extended from one (1) to two (2) Business Days, creating a contradiction with previous mentions. For example, this will contradict Section 2.1(b)(i) which indicate 'notify each Party at least fifteen (15) days (or as much notice as is reasonably possible) in advance of each JSC meeting', this contradiction is because there is no way someone could send the notification via mail, as it takes 10 days for the mail to deliver the notification. Making some clauses unreachable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Committee Chair. The JSC will be co-chaired by a Pfizer JSC Member and an Exact JSC Member (each, a "JSC Co-Chair"). Each Party may replace its JSC Co-Chair at any time upon notice to the other Party. The role of secretary of the JSC shall rotate each meeting between the JSC Co-Chairs (or any JSC Member who is appointed, by mutual agreement of both JSC Co-Chairs, as secretary of the JSC). The secretary of the JSC shall: (i) notify each Party at least fifteen (15) days (or as much notice as is reasonably possible) in advance of each JSC meeting; (ii) collect and organize agenda items from each Party for each JSC meeting; (iii) prepare and circulate to JSC Members each JSC meeting agenda no later than five (5) Business Days (or as far in advance as is reasonably possible) prior to the scheduled date for each JSC meeting; and (iv) prepare the written minutes of each JSC meeting and, within fifteen (15) days after such meeting, circulate such minutes for review and approval by the Parties.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 9.5 increases the delivery time for notices sent by mail to ten (10) days. This creates a direct contradiction with Section (b)(i), which requires fifteen (15) days' notice for JSC meetings. Because mail delivery now takes ten days, it's impossible to comply with the fifteen-day notice requirement using mail, creating an inconsistency regarding compliance, rendering the fifteen-day notice impossible to fulfill by mail.
